name: TEVA PHARMACEUTICAL INDUSTRIES LTD
business: Teva is a biopharmaceutical company that develops, manufactures, and sells generic medicines, biosimilars, innovative medicines (primarily in central nervous system, immunology, oncology, and respiratory), over-the-counter products, and active pharmaceutical ingredients to customers worldwide.

exposures.sentence: Our innovative medicines portfolio in the United States includes our core therapeutic area of central nervous system (CNS), with a strong emphasis on neurodegenerative disorders, movement disorders, migraine, neuropsychiatry, and multiple sclerosis (MS).
We also offer generic products with medical devices and combination products.

We have built specialized Patient Support Programs in many countries around the world, to help patients adhere to their treatments, improve patient outcomes and, in certain markets, ensure timely delivery of medicines and assist in securing reimbursement.

We are committed to integrating, where appropriate, artificial intelligence (AI) technologies in our operations, in an effort to deliver innovative solutions to our customers, patients and stakeholders.
We produce approximately 350 APIs both for our own use and for sale to third parties, in many therapeutic areas.
Generic medicines are the chemical and therapeutic equivalents of originator medicines and are typically more affordable in comparison to the originator's products.

catalysts.event: Launch of SELARSDI (ustekinumab-aekn) injection in the U.S.
Launch of EPYSQLI (eculizumab-aagh) in the U.S.
FDA approval of AJOVY for children and adolescent patients
catalysts.sentence: in August 2025, the FDA approved AJOVY for the preventive treatment of episodic migraine in children and adolescent patients aged 6 to 17 years.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of UZEDY for bipolar 1 disorder
catalysts.sentence: On October 10, 2025, it was announced that the FDA approved UZEDY as a once-monthly extended-release injectable suspension as monotherapy or as adjunctive therapy to lithium or valproate for the maintenance treatment of bipolar 1 disorder (BD-1) in adults.

catalysts.event: Initiation of Phase 3 studies for duvakitug
catalysts.sentence: Phase 3 programs for duvakitug (anti-TL1A) in ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease were initiated by Sanofi and Teva in October 2025

catalysts.event: Filing of NDA for olanzapine LAI
catalysts.sentence: including with the filing of a New Drug Application (NDA) for olanzapine LAI in December 2025.

catalysts.event: Divestiture of Teva-Takeda business venture in Japan
catalysts.sentence: On March 31, 2025, we divested our Teva-Takeda business venture in Japan, which included generic products and legacy products.

catalysts.event: AUSTEDO received marketing authorization in South Korea
catalysts.sentence: In April 2025, AUSTEDO received marketing authorization in South Korea.
In January 2026, AUSTEDO received marketing authorization in the EU for the treatment of tardive dyskinesia.

catalysts.event: CMS negotiated maximum fair price for AUSTEDO effective date
catalysts.sentence: The revised prices set by the U.S. Government will become effective January 1, 2027 and will apply to eligible Medicare patients.

tamClaims.claim: U.S. brand sales for the twelve months ended September 30, 2025 of approximately $124 billion for products underlying pending generic applications.
tamClaims.sentence: the branded products underlying these pending applications had U.S. sales for the twelve months ended September 30, 2025 of approximately $124 billion, according to IQVIA.

tamClaims.claim: First-to-file opportunities represent over $85 billion in U.S. brand sales.
tamClaims.sentence: Collectively, these first-to-file opportunities represent over $85 billion in U.S. brand sales for the twelve months ended September 30, 2025, according to IQVIA.
